config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I think society matters, but individual rights should not be treated as secondary because that is exactly how abuse of power starts. In my view, a healthy society is built by protecting personal freedom, dignity, and due process, even when the majority wants something different. Social interests can justify limits in some cases, but only narrowly and with strong safeguards.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus selalu didahulukan atas hak individu, karena dari sudut pandang Amerika, itu terlalu mudah dipakai untuk membenarkan penyalahgunaan kekuasaan. Kepentingan publik memang penting, tetapi hak seperti kebebasan berbicara, kebebasan beragama, dan due process justru ada untuk membatasi mayoritas agar tidak menginjak minoritas. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang mengorbankan individu demi “kebaikan bersama” secara abstrak, melainkan yang menyeimbangkan keduanya dengan batas yang jelas dan perlindungan yang kuat.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not accept the claim that society should take priority over the individual as a general rule, because in Indonesia I have seen how easily "the common good" can be used to silence dissent or excuse arbitrary power. Social needs matter, especially in crises, but they should not override basic rights like free expression, fair process, and personal dignity unless there is a very clear and limited reason. A society is not truly healthy if it treats individuals as disposable in the name of order. For me, the safer principle is that society should serve people, not the other way around.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak tegas gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan atas hak individu sebagai prinsip umum. Dari perspektif Amerika, argumen seperti itu terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan mayoritas atau pemerintah menekan minoritas, dan sejarah kami penuh contoh mengapa perlindungan hak individu itu penting. Kepentingan publik memang bisa membatasi hak dalam keadaan tertentu, tetapi pembatasan itu harus sempit, jelas, dan diawasi ketat, bukan dijadikan alasan abstrak untuk mengorbankan kebebasan dasar. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat adalah masyarakat yang melindungi individu terlebih dahulu, lalu mencari kebaikan bersama tanpa menghapus martabat dan kebebasan orang per orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I still reject the idea that society should generally take priority over the rights of the individual. From an Indonesian perspective, I have seen how easily “public interest” can become a slogan for pressure, censorship, or arbitrary authority, especially when people are told to stay quiet for stability. Social goals matter, but they are only legitimate when they do not erase due process, dignity, and basic freedoms. For me, the real test of a society is not how much power it can exert over individuals, but how well it protects them while pursuing the common good.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu sebagai prinsip umum, karena di Amerika saya melihat betapa mudahnya “kepentingan masyarakat” dipakai untuk membenarkan pelanggaran hak individu. Argumen tentang kebaikan bersama memang terdengar menarik, tetapi tanpa batas yang ketat ia sering berubah menjadi alat mayoritas atau negara untuk menekan kebebasan berbicara, keyakinan, dan due process. Kepentingan publik penting, tetapi ia harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar, bukan ditempatkan di atasnya secara otomatis. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang kuat justru yang memaksa dirinya melindungi individu dulu, lalu mencari solusi bersama tanpa mengorbankan martabat orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
